hero.tagline: India ka electric SUV. Built for Bharat.

baasScheme.description: Battery-as-a-Service (BaaS) — buy the car without the battery pack at a lower upfront price, pay per km for battery usage.

overview.minides: India's largest carmaker enters EVs — the e Vitara is Maruti's first electric SUV, offering long range, BaaS flexibility, and Maruti's unmatched service network.
overview.description: The Maruti Suzuki e Vitara is the brand's first electric SUV, launched via Nexa showrooms in February 2026. Built on the Heartect-e platform co-developed with Toyota, it is offered in three variants — Delta (49 kWh), Zeta (61 kWh), and Alpha (61 kWh) — plus an Alpha Dual Tone option. The 49 kWh battery powers a 142 bhp FWD motor with 440 km ARAI range, while the 61 kWh battery delivers 172 bhp with 543 km ARAI range. Both batteries use LFP blade cells sourced from BYD. Standard features include a 10.25-inch touchscreen, 10.25-inch digital cluster, 7 airbags, Level 2 ADAS, wireless Android Auto & Apple CarPlay. Top trims add ventilated front seats, 360-degree camera, panoramic sunroof, Infinity sound system, and 19-inch alloys. The e Vitara also offers an innovative Battery-as-a-Service (BaaS) subscription scheme. It is India's first electric SUV to receive a 5-star Bharat NCAP rating.
